label: SKM Power*Tools (PTW) - Arc Flash Evaluation report/spreadsheet
toolVersionRange: PTW ~6.5-9.x Arc Flash Evaluation output table. Column captions verified against two real SKM-generated study reports: e-Hazard sample study (SKM PowerTools 9.0, IEEE 1584-2018, 2020) and Brady sample risk assessment (SKM 9.0.05, 2021). The interactive AF spreadsheet export in the customer's PTW version may word captions slightly differently - confirm against one real export.
policyNote: LIABILITY POSTURE: ServiceCycle stores PE-stamped study RESULTS; it never computes incident energy and never asserts PPE categories. SKM's computed PPE/hazard-category column ('PPE Level / Notes (*N)', 'Required Protective FR Clothing Category') is deliberately mapped to NOTHING by this template. Unit conversions here are mechanical only (kV to V, seconds to ms); no engineering derivation is performed.
sourceNotes: PRIMARY (verified): 'Incident Energy Summary Report' table in the e-Hazard Arc-Flash-Study-Report-Example.pdf (generated by SKM PowerTools 9.0, IEEE 1584-2018) - columns A-S read directly: Bus Name / Protective Device Name / Bus kV / Bus Bolted Fault (kA) / Bus Arcing Fault (kA) / Prot Dev Bolted Fault (kA) / Prot Dev Arcing Fault (kA) / Trip/Delay Time (sec.) / Breaker Opening Time/Tol (sec.) / Equip Type / Electrode Config / Box Width (in) / Box Height (in) / Box Depth (in) / Gap (mm) / Arc Flash Boundary (in) / Working Distance (in) / Incident Energy (cal/cm2) / PPE Level / Notes (*N).

SECONDARY (verified): Brady 'Arc Flash Risk Assessment' sample report (SKM 9.0.05) Section 6 heading list - same columns with punctuation variants ('Prot. Dev. Bolted Fault (kA)', 'Breaker Opening Time/Tol. (sec.)') plus a 'Ground' column.
SKM KB 'Export/Import in Excel' (support.skm.com/KB/View/171515) documents the tab-delimited project export; that export is the INPUT model, not this arc-flash RESULTS table.

mappings.source: Column A of the SKM 9.0 Incident Energy Summary Report (e-Hazard sample); same heading in Brady/SKM 9.0.05 report.
Column B of the SKM 9.0 Incident Energy Summary Report; Brady report heading 2.
mappings.note: This is the clearing device's NAME/designation, not a catalog model number. Mapped to deviceModel for parity with the existing AFX crosswalk, which maps ARCAD's 'Upstream protection device' the same way.

mappings.source: Column C of the SKM 9.0 Incident Energy Summary Report (values like 0.208, 13.8); Brady report heading 3.
Column D of the SKM 9.0 Incident Energy Summary Report; Brady report heading 4.
mappings.note: Bus-level bolted fault. The device-side 'Prot Dev Bolted Fault (kA)' column is intentionally NOT mapped (AFX carries one bus-level value).

mappings.source: Column E of the SKM 9.0 Incident Energy Summary Report; Brady report heading 5.
Column H of the SKM 9.0 Incident Energy Summary Report; Brady report heading 8.
mappings.note: SKM reports device trip/delay time and breaker opening time as SEPARATE columns. This maps trip/delay only (s x 1000 = ms). When the 'Breaker Opening Time/Tol' column holds a value > 0, a per-row warning is raised (see rowChecks) because total clearing time = trip + opening; ServiceCycle does not sum columns.

mappings.source: Column J of the SKM 9.0 Incident Energy Summary Report (values like PNL, SWG, MCC); Brady report heading 11 ('Equip. Type').
mappings.note: SKM abbreviations (PNL/SWG/MCC) pass through as free text; ServiceCycle's importer maps equipment-type strings with a documented conservative default.

mappings.source: Column K of the SKM 9.0 Incident Energy Summary Report (values VCB etc.; IEEE 1584-2018 reports only).
mappings.note: Present only in IEEE 1584-2018 era reports; absent from the 2021 Brady heading list (1584-2002 style).

mappings.source: Column O of the SKM 9.0 Incident Energy Summary Report; Brady report heading 12.
Column P of the SKM 9.0 Incident Energy Summary Report; Brady report heading 13 ('Arc Flash Boundary (in)').
Column Q of the SKM 9.0 Incident Energy Summary Report; Brady report heading 14.
Column R of the SKM 9.0 Incident Energy Summary Report; Brady report heading 15.
mappings.note: SKM prints threshold cells like '< 1.2' for below-threshold rows; those cells are not a number and are surfaced as per-row issues for engineer review instead of being silently coerced.

ignoredByPolicy.note: Column S of the SKM report is the software-computed PPE level. Recorded product policy: ServiceCycle never asserts PPE categories; the tool-computed PPE/hazard-category column is mapped to nothing. ('Required Protective FR Clothing Category' is the older 1584-2002-era SKM caption for the same concept.)

knownUnmapped.note: Device-side fault-current duplicates of the bus-level columns (differ when multiple sources feed the fault). AFX v1 carries the bus-level values only; kept visible here so they do not show up as 'unknown'.
Column I. Separate breaker-mechanism opening time. Not mapped: AFX has a single clearingTimeMs and ServiceCycle does not sum columns. A per-row warning fires when this is > 0 (see rowChecks).
System-grounding flag (Brady/SKM heading 10). AFX v1 has no grounding field; listed so it is recognized rather than 'unknown'.
Columns L-N: enclosure dimensions. AFX uses an enclosure CLASS (enclosureType), not dimensions, so these are recognized but not mapped (same stance as the existing ARCAD crosswalk note).

rowChecks.message: Breaker opening time > 0: AFX clearingTimeMs was set from Trip/Delay Time only and UNDERSTATES total clearing time for this row. Verify the total clearing time with the study engineer.
